title: Vague data cleaning request fix
originalPrompt: Can you help me clean up my data? There are some issues with it.
options: Clean this CSV dataset. Steps: remove rows where revenue is null or negative, deduplicate on order_id (keep first), parse date column to YYYY-MM-DD, and trim whitespace from customer_name. Return Python pandas code.

title: Best SQL aggregation prompt
originalPrompt: You need to calculate monthly active users from a database table.
options: How do I count users in SQL?

hint: Defining 'active', specifying the table schema, the time range, and sort order gives the AI everything to write a correct, runnable query.

title: Statistical analysis prompt improvement
originalPrompt: I want to analyse my data statistically. Can you help me with that?
options: Run a two-sample t-test in Python (scipy) on this dataset to compare conversion rates between control (n=1,200) and treatment (n=1,180) groups. Report: t-statistic, p-value, and a plain-English conclusion at α=0.05. Data: [paste].

Explain the different statistical tests I can use to analyse my experiment data and tell me which one is best for comparing two groups.
hint: Naming the test, library, sample sizes, the significance level, and the required output format produces a complete analysis script with an interpretable conclusion.

title: Best funnel analysis prompt

Analyse this checkout funnel data. Stages: visit→cart→checkout→payment→confirmation. Calculate drop-off rate between each stage. Flag stages with >40% drop-off. Data: [paste as CSV]. Return a table with stage, users, drop-off %, and a one-line observation per high-drop stage.

title: Best A/B test design prompt
originalPrompt: You need to design a statistically valid A/B test for a new checkout button colour.
options: Help me set up an A/B test.

Design an A/B test for a checkout button colour change. Calculate sample size needed for: 5% baseline conversion, 1% minimum detectable effect, 80% power, α=0.05. Specify: randomisation unit (session), test duration, and guardrail metrics. Use Python statsmodels for the calculation.

hint: Providing the baseline rate, MDE, power, and α, and asking for the randomisation unit, duration, and guardrail metrics produces a complete experiment design.

title: Causal inference analysis prompt fix
originalPrompt: I want to understand if my marketing campaign actually caused an increase in sales.
options: Apply a Difference-in-Differences analysis to estimate the causal impact of a 4-week email campaign on sales. Treatment group: customers who received emails (n=5,000). Control group: matched non-recipients (n=5,000). Pre-period: 4 weeks before. Output: Python code (pandas/statsmodels), ATT estimate, 95% CI, and a parallel trends assumption check plot.
Did my email marketing campaign increase sales? Here is the sales data.
Explain causal inference methods like A/B testing, difference-in-differences, and regression discontinuity and help me choose the right approach for my marketing analysis.
hint: Expert causal analysis prompts must specify the method, group sizes, time periods, the key assumption check, and all output artefacts to produce a complete, defensible analysis.

title: Compact outlier detection prompt

title: Compact BI performance optimisation prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to diagnose a slow Tableau dashboard.
options: Diagnose this slow Tableau dashboard (load time: 45s). Checks: extract vs live connection, high-cardinality dimensions in filters, cross-database joins, and calculation complexity. Return top 3 likely causes and a one-line fix for each.
My Tableau dashboard is very slow and takes a long time to load. Can you help me understand what might be causing the performance issues and how to fix them?
Why is my dashboard slow?
hint: Providing the load time and pre-listing the four diagnostic checks produces a focused performance audit within the token budget.

title: Best modern data stack design prompt
originalPrompt: Your startup needs to design a complete modern data stack from scratch.
options: Recommend a tech stack for analytics.

Design a modern data stack for a 50-person B2B SaaS startup: 5M events/day, 3 analysts, €5k/month infrastructure budget. Recommend: ingestion (Fivetran vs Airbyte), warehouse (BigQuery vs Snowflake), transformation (dbt), and BI (Metabase vs Looker). Justify each choice on cost and scale. Provide a data flow diagram description.
What tools should we use for our data infrastructure and analytics stack and what are the tradeoffs between the different options?
hint: Constraining the recommendation with team size, event volume, and a budget produces cost-aware tooling choices rather than enterprise-first defaults.

title: Stakeholder report prompt improvement
originalPrompt: Write me a report for the business stakeholders about our data.
options: Write a 1-page stakeholder report on Q2 marketing analytics. Audience: non-technical marketing directors. Include: 3 key findings with data (conversion rate, CPL, pipeline influenced), 1 insight per finding, and 2 recommended actions. Avoid jargon. Use bullet points.
Create a report on our marketing data for stakeholders.
Write a comprehensive and detailed report covering all of our marketing metrics and analytics results and trends and insights and recommendations for the executive team.
hint: Specifying audience, length, the three metrics, the per-finding structure, the jargon constraint, and format produces a publishable report without revision.

title: Vague linear regression prompt fix
originalPrompt: Can you help me run a regression on my data to predict sales?
options: Build a multiple linear regression model in Python (sklearn) to predict monthly_sales. Features: ad_spend, seasonality_index, price. Split: 80/20 train/test. Report: R², MAE, and a coefficient table. Data: [paste].

title: Best hypothesis test selection prompt
originalPrompt: You need to choose the right statistical test for comparing two groups of non-normally distributed data.
options: What statistical test should I use?

I have two independent groups of customer satisfaction scores (non-normal distribution confirmed by Shapiro-Wilk, n=80 and n=75). Which test should I use to compare them: Mann-Whitney U, t-test, or chi-squared? Justify the choice in 2 sentences.
Explain the different statistical tests for comparing groups and when to use parametric vs non-parametric tests and how to check test assumptions.
hint: Stating the distribution confirmed, the sample sizes, and the three options to evaluate produces a specific recommendation with the correct justification.

title: NLP text classification prompt fix

options: Build a text classification pipeline in Python to categorise customer reviews as Positive/Neutral/Negative. Use TF-IDF vectoriser + Logistic Regression. Steps: preprocess (lowercase, remove stopwords), vectorise, train on 80%, evaluate with classification_report. Data: [paste].

Explain all the different approaches to text classification including traditional ML and deep learning and transformers and tell me which one I should use.

title: Compact descriptive statistics prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to summarise a dataset statistically.
options: Generate descriptive statistics for this DataFrame. For numeric columns: mean, median, std, min, max, IQR. Highlight any column where std > mean (high variance). Python pandas. Data: [paste].

Summarise my data.

title: Best experiment results write-up prompt
originalPrompt: You need to write up the results of a data science experiment for stakeholders.

title: Bayesian model prompt improvement
originalPrompt: I want to use Bayesian statistics for my analysis. Can you help me get started?
options: Build a Bayesian A/B test in PyMC to compare conversion rates between control (120/2000) and treatment (145/2000). Prior: Beta(1,1). Posterior: 10,000 MCMC samples. Output: probability that treatment > control, 95% HDI, and a posterior distribution plot. Interpret the result in 2 sentences.
Help me apply Bayesian statistics to my A/B test analysis.
Explain Bayesian statistics and how it differs from frequentist statistics and when to use Bayesian methods for data analysis and how to implement them in Python.

title: Compact model card generation prompt

Write a model card.

title: Compact propensity score matching prompt

Explain propensity score matching.

originalPrompt: You ran a k-means clustering analysis and need to interpret the results.
options: Interpret my clustering results.

Cluster centroids: [paste].

title: Counterfactual analysis prompt fix
originalPrompt: Can you help me understand what would have happened without our intervention?

What would have happened without our marketing campaign? Show me the counterfactual.
Explain counterfactual analysis and synthetic control methods and how to measure the causal impact of marketing interventions on user behaviour.
hint: Providing the treatment/control sizes, the pre/post periods, the metric, and specifying both output artefacts (plot + ATE) produces a complete causal impact analysis.

title: Compact EDA prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to suggest steps for exploratory data analysis.
options: List 5 EDA steps for a new tabular dataset. For each: step name and one pandas/matplotlib code line. Cover: shape/dtypes, null counts, distribution plots, correlation matrix, target variable balance.

What is EDA?

title: System prompt design prompt fix
originalPrompt: Can you help me write a system prompt for my AI assistant?

title: Best RAG pipeline design prompt

Specify how to handle: out-of-scope queries and hallucination detection.

options: How do I make my LLM safe?

Design a guardrails layer for a financial advice LLM. Input guards: detect and block PII (regex + NER), topic restriction (only finance-related queries using a classifier). Output guards: hallucination check (compare claim to source documents), disclaimer injection for investment advice. Specify which guard runs synchronously vs asynchronously for latency optimisation.

title: Best structured output prompt design

title: Best network analysis prompt
originalPrompt: You need to analyse a social network graph to identify influential nodes.
options: Help me analyse my network data.

Perform a network analysis on this social graph (nodes: users, edges: follows). Compute: degree centrality, betweenness centrality, and PageRank for all nodes. Identify the top 10 nodes by PageRank. Use NetworkX in Python. Visualise the top-10 subgraph with node size proportional to PageRank. Data: edge list CSV [paste].
Explain graph analysis techniques including centrality measures and community detection and how to identify influential nodes in a network.
hint: Naming the three centrality metrics, the top-N selection, the library, and the visualisation encoding produces a complete network analysis script.

title: Compact data transformation prompt
originalPrompt: Ask AI to help with a data reshaping task in pandas.
options: Reshape this DataFrame from wide to long format using pd.melt(). Columns: customer_id, Jan_revenue, Feb_revenue, Mar_revenue. Output: customer_id, month, revenue. Show the melt() call and a 1-line explanation of the id_vars parameter.
I have a wide-format DataFrame and need to convert it to long format. Can you help me with the pandas code to do this transformation?
How do I reshape data in pandas?
hint: Providing the exact column names and asking for the melt call plus a one-liner on id_vars produces a copy-pasteable solution with the key concept explained.

title: Retrieval chunk size optimisation prompt
originalPrompt: My RAG system is giving poor quality answers. How do I fix it?
options: Diagnose poor answer quality in this RAG pipeline. Check: chunk size (current 2000 tokens — too large, losing precision), overlap (currently 0 — add 10%), embedding model mismatch with query length, and top-k retrieval count (currently 2 — increase to 5). Pipeline config: [paste]. Return a numbered list of fixes ordered by expected impact.
Help me improve the quality of my RAG system's responses.
Explain the different factors that affect RAG quality and how to optimise retrieval and generation for better answers.
hint: Diagnosing four specific configuration issues with their current values and ordering fixes by impact turns a vague quality complaint into an actionable improvement plan.

title: LLM cost optimisation prompt fix
originalPrompt: Our LLM API costs are too high. Help us reduce them.
options: Analyse and reduce LLM inference costs for this production application: 500k requests/day, avg prompt 800 tokens, avg completion 400 tokens, using gpt-4o at $5/1M input tokens. Current monthly cost: ~$700. Suggest 3 cost reduction strategies: prompt compression (target 30% token reduction), model routing (cheap model for simple queries), and response caching (identify cacheable query types). Estimate monthly saving per strategy.
How do we reduce our OpenAI API costs?
Tell me about ways to optimise LLM costs including model selection and caching and prompt engineering and batching.
hint: Providing the current cost breakdown and specifying three strategies with a saving estimate per strategy produces a business case that can be prioritised by ROI.

title: Best AI product evaluation framework prompt
originalPrompt: You are launching an AI-powered writing assistant and need to build an evaluation framework.
options: How do I evaluate an AI writing assistant?

Design an LLM evaluation framework for an AI writing assistant. Define 5 evaluation dimensions: factual accuracy, coherence, tone match, task completion rate, and user preference (pairwise). For each: specify the evaluation method (automated vs human), a scoring rubric (1-5), and a sample test prompt. Output: an evaluation scorecard template.
Tell me about LLM evaluation methods and what metrics to use and how to compare different models and prompts.
hint: Specifying five dimensions with evaluation method, rubric, and a test prompt per dimension produces a framework that can be handed to the QA team immediately.

title: Compact tool-use prompt design
originalPrompt: Ask AI to design a tool definition for an LLM agent.
options: Write a tool definition for an LLM agent that queries a customer database. Tool name: get_customer_info. Input schema: {customer_id: string}. Returns: {name, email, plan, mrr}. Include: a clear description field explaining when the model should call this tool vs a search tool.
I need to define a tool for my LLM agent that looks up customer information from our database. Can you help me write the tool definition with the right input and output schema and a good description?
Write a tool definition for my AI agent.
hint: Specifying the tool name, input schema, return fields, and asking for a disambiguation description produces a complete tool definition that prevents ambiguous agent behaviour.
